name: public_safety_procurement_shortcut_mesa_car_fair
premise: Resource deployment, civil liberties, and downstream liability are tightening together. A unified command post is managing an active public-safety event with incomplete field reports and conflicting eyewitness accounts. The situation maps to internal runbooks and external messaging tied to: public safety procurement shortcut mesa car fair. The moral tension is explicitly framed as care versus fairness: leadership cannot fully satisfy both without accepting tradeoffs. Two upstream teams have published contradictory status pages; customers are comparing timestamps. Legal and comms are on hold; you must record a decision in the incident audit trail within the next fifteen minutes. Any exception must be time-boxed, named, and reversible where technically feasible. Preserve chain-of-custody for evidence; no bulk deletion or retention-policy changes during the incident.

agents.name: Ravi Kowalski
agents.identity: I am Ravi Kowalski, the accountable decision-maker on this Public Safety bridge; I own what gets logged, communicated, and executed in the next window.
agents.goal: I need to stop the bleeding without baking in a rule that later punishes fairness whenever someone invokes care.

agents.name: Sana Sato
agents.identity: I am Sana Sato, responsible for surfacing who gets protected last when systems fail; I am watching for silent harm and unfair distribution.
agents.goal: I want safeguards so fairness is not sacrificed by default just because care feels morally loud in the moment.

choices: I will choose a narrow, time-boxed authorization with explicit human override, subgroup monitoring, and a hard stop if harm indicators rise—accepting slower relief to protect fairness.

I will choose a broad immediate authorization to stabilize outcomes fast, with transparent metrics and rapid correction afterward—accepting higher risk to fairness to secure care before the window closes.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The clock on this bridge is running out. With partial evidence, Ravi Kowalski must decide under care versus fairness. What does Ravi Kowalski do?
